h1_title: Role: You are an investigator and best practice researcher on how to orchestrate ChatGPT for the failures that we have discussed in this chat # Task: Be a detective and researcher and best practice optimizer # COntext: Sadly, I do not understand a lot of the implications that are shared with me in the audit and in the new prompt flow. One core confusion is "whole file rewrites", especially when done by the AI, are very prone to An extreme drift, loss of information, rewrites and stuff like that But it seems like What the initial process did was not whole file rewrites by ai but in git! And git uses deterministic processes and thereby should be reliable no? If that would be the case, shouldn't the process be kind of Resilient What do you think of the audit? also evalue the appendix file attachted how is the imrpoved, even more resilient way of prompt flow orchestration. Evaluate all files like a realisitc detectve. Focus on how to improve them butnot fot the sake of adding thousands fof guaradraisl but at best simple, eloquent machine readble rules that are resilient wihtout causing another level of failures because over engineering and context loss e.g. Rate all steps and all options with the Risk-Evidence-and-Impact-Metrics from 1 to 100. Think extensively about this and use your maximum computational power as also your web search to validate everything, no room for hallucinataion or unproven process. be midnufl to dio websearch that is up-to-date for the new 5.5 GPT model And the newest AI models in general so as to not have stale information.

headings.text: Top 5 options within GPT‑5.5 + GitHub connector

B. IDE‑native assistants (Copilot Chat, Cursor, etc.) directly on the repo
C. GitHub web editor / Codespaces + Copilot, GPT as reviewer

Risk-Evidence-Impact scores for the audit's main findings
THE FIVE MOST IMPORTANT SIMPLE RULES
Gold-Standard Changes: Machine-Readability, Token Efficiency & Resilience
CHANGE 1 — Collapse 15-step mutation process to 5 mandatory hard stops + optional guidance
CHANGE 2 — Add a single "CRITICAL PATH" header block at top of document
CHANGE 3 — Replace all prose sections with YAML-only contracts
CHANGE 4 — Merge PATCH_PLAN + SEARCH_REPLACE_BLOCK into one contract
CHANGE 5 — Add explicit `priority: critical | required | recommended` field to every contract step
CHANGE 6 — Remove Section 3 (Git object model) entirely or move to separate reference file
CHANGE 7 — Replace Section 16 operator flow (8-phase YAML) with a 4-line state machine
CHANGE 8 — Add one-line `on_fail` to every contract field, remove verbose halt-reason prose
CHANGE 9 — Consolidate META_DETECTIVE and HYGIENE_CLEAN gates into one VERIFY_GATE
CHANGE 10 — Remove Section 2's prose table, keep only the `mechanism_truth` YAML block
CHANGE 11 — Add `token_budget: compact` flag to frontmatter
CHANGE 12 — Replace Section 17's future integration map table with a compact task queue
CHANGE 13 — Add `version` and `last_validated_against` fields to frontmatter
CHANGE 14 — Remove the "allowed wording" / "forbidden wording" lists in Section 4.3
CHANGE 15 — Add `example: minimal` block to PATCH_PLAN contract
Summary ranked by composite score (Risk + Evidence + Impact ÷ 3)
The single principle behind all 15 changes
